FHA Closing Costs

FHA closing costs in Florida

Closing costs are the fees and prepaid items you pay to finalize your loan, separate from your down payment. On an FHA loan in Florida, they typically run about 2%–5% of the purchase price — but several tools can reduce what you pay out of pocket.

What's typically included

Common closing costs include lender/origination fees, the FHA appraisal, title insurance and settlement fees, recording and government charges, and prepaid items like homeowners insurance and property taxes set up in escrow. The 1.75% upfront FHA mortgage insurance is usually financed into the loan rather than paid at closing.

Who can pay them

This is where FHA helps buyers: sellers can contribute up to 6% of the price toward your closing costs, you can use gift funds, and lenders can offer credits (often in exchange for a slightly higher rate). Florida down payment assistance programs may also cover closing costs.

Estimating your costs

On a $350,000 Florida home, expect roughly $7,000–$17,500 in closing costs before any seller credits or assistance. Use our FHA calculator to estimate the payment, and ask us for a detailed cost breakdown for your specific scenario.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

How much are FHA closing costs in Florida?+
Typically about 2%–5% of the purchase price, covering lender fees, appraisal, title, government charges, and prepaid taxes/insurance. Seller credits and assistance can reduce your out-of-pocket amount.
Can the seller pay my FHA closing costs?+
Yes. On an FHA loan, sellers can contribute up to 6% of the purchase price toward your closing costs and prepaids.
Is the upfront FHA mortgage insurance part of closing costs?+
The 1.75% upfront MIP is usually financed into your loan balance rather than paid in cash at closing.
Can I use gift funds for closing costs?+
Yes, gift funds from family can be applied to closing costs, and Florida down payment assistance programs may help as well.
